features.name: Macromolecule Detection & Notation Refinement
features.description: Automatically detects macromolecule semantic types (DNA, RNA, peptides) in columns and refines their notation (HELM, FASTA, BILN, separator) using configurable detectors.
features.evidence_summary: All share the macromolecule semTypeDetector / notationRefiner role tags and the detect/refine naming pattern; the Bio guide doc covers detection as a foundational capability.

features.name: Sequence Cell Rendering
features.description: Renders macromolecule sequence columns in the Datagrok grid with notation-specific cell renderers (FASTA, HELM, BILN, separator, custom) and per-column tooltips and header panels.
features.evidence_summary: All carry the cellRenderer role tag and are named after the notation they render; longSeqTable* functions are dev test beds for long-sequence rendering in each notation.

features.name: Sequence Difference Rendering
features.description: Compares two macromolecule sequence columns to build a per-position difference representation and renders it via a dedicated difference cell renderer.
features.evidence_summary: MacromoleculeDifferenceCellRenderer and Compare Sequences share explicit naming and description around per-position sequence comparison; sequenceAlignment provides the underlying pairwise alignment.

features.name: Notation Conversion
features.description: Converts macromolecule columns between supported sequence notations (FASTA, HELM, BILN, separator, custom) via an interactive conversion dialog.
features.evidence_summary: Convert Notation and convertDialog share the same name root and dialog entry point; the Polytool doc covers multi-notation polymer workflows that this feature enables.

features.name: Molecule to HELM Conversion
features.description: Converts small-molecule structures (molfiles / SDF) to HELM notation by matching atoms against a monomer library, enabling peptide enumeration workflows.
features.evidence_summary: Molecules to HELM, Molecule to HELM Single, getMolFromHelm, and molToHelmConverterPy all implement the molecule-to-HELM pipeline; SDF to JSON Library converts SDF monomer inputs for this workflow.

features.name: Atomic Level Structure
features.description: Expands macromolecule sequences (HELM / FASTA / BILN) into full atomic-level molblocks and displays 2D / 3D molecular structures in the property panel.
features.evidence_summary: All share the 'To Atomic Level' / seq2atomic name root and the same pipeline of converting macromolecule sequences to molblocks for 2D / 3D visualization; demoBioAtomicLevel is the demo entry point.

features.name: File Import / Export (FASTA, BAM)
features.description: Imports FASTA / nucleotide files and BAM alignment files directly into Datagrok tables and exports sequence columns back to FASTA format.
features.evidence_summary: importFasta, importBam, and saveAsFasta carry fileHandler / fileExporter roles; oligo-toolkit and oligonucleotide-design docs cover the nucleotide-file import use case.

features.name: Monomer Library Management
features.description: Provides a full CRUD UI to manage HELM monomer libraries and collections, including browsing, editing monomers, matching molecules to monomers, and standardising library JSON.
features.evidence_summary: All share 'Monomer' / 'Library' name roots or the Manage Monomer Libraries menu path, covering the full lifecycle of HELM monomer library management from browse to edit to standardise.

features.name: Split to Monomers
features.description: Splits a macromolecule sequence column into individual per-position monomer columns, enabling position-level analysis of peptide libraries.
features.evidence_summary: Split to Monomers and SplitToMonomersEditor share the same name and editor role; getHelmMonomers provides per-column monomer extraction used by the split operation.

features.name: Get Region
features.description: Extracts a named subsequence region (e.g. CDR loop, variable domain) from a macromolecule column into a new column, via an interactive panel, top-menu action, or region input widget.
features.evidence_summary: All share the 'Get Region' name root or describe sub-region extraction; Sequence Column Input exposes region selection as a reusable input widget; *App functions are dev test entry points.

features.name: WebLogo & Composition Analysis
features.description: Visualizes the positional monomer composition of a sequence set as a WebLogo plot, with aggregate and per-row composition panels.
features.evidence_summary: WebLogo, both Composition Analysis variants, and the two demo apps all share the WebLogo / composition naming; the help doc directly documents this viewer.

features.name: Multiple Sequence Alignment
features.description: Aligns a set of macromolecule sequences using built-in engine or PepSeA (MAFFT-based) alignment algorithms, including support for non-canonical peptides and HELM.
features.evidence_summary: MSA, Multiple Sequence Alignment, and PepSeA all carry sequenceMSA roles; read-tree-pkl reads phylogenetic clustering trees produced alongside MSA; Sequence generator creates synthetic test data.

features.name: Sequence Space
features.description: Projects macromolecule sequences into a 2D scatter plot via dimensionality reduction of pairwise distances, enabling visual exploration of chemical diversity.
features.evidence_summary: Sequence Space, sequenceSpaceTransform, and SequenceSpaceEditor implement the dim-reduction pipeline; Encode Sequences carries the 'dim-red-preprocessing-function' role; Embed script does the numeric embedding; demoBioSequenceSpace is the demo.

features.name: Sequence Activity Cliffs
features.description: Detects pairs of macromolecule sequences that are structurally similar but have a large difference in a measured biological activity, highlighting SAR discontinuities.
features.evidence_summary: All five function IDs share the 'seqActivityCliffs' / 'Sequence Activity Cliffs' name; Peptides SAR doc covers the SAR / activity cliff analysis workflow for macromolecules.

features.name: Sequence Similarity & Diversity Search
features.description: Interactive viewers to find the most similar sequences to a query and to pick the most diverse subset from a macromolecule dataset.
features.evidence_summary: The four viewer functions pair by name (Similarity Search / similaritySearch, Diversity Search / diversitySearch); the demo unifies both into one entry point.

features.name: Subsequence Search & Substructure Filter
features.description: Searches for a motif or subsequence within macromolecule columns and provides a grid filter widget to interactively narrow rows by substructure match.
features.evidence_summary: Subsequence Search / SearchSubsequenceEditor implement motif search; Bio Substructure Filter provides the interactive filter widget using the same underlying matcher.

features.name: Sequence Identity & Similarity Scoring
features.description: Computes per-row identity or monomer-fingerprint similarity scores relative to a reference sequence, adding scored result columns to the table.
features.evidence_summary: Identity, Similarity, and seqIdentity all compute pairwise sequence scores against a reference; Helm Fingerprints generates the monomer-fingerprint vectors consumed by the Similarity scorer.

features.name: Antibody Numbering & Annotations
features.description: Assigns standard antibody numbering schemes (IMGT, Kabat, Chothia, AHo) to sequence columns and lets users view and manage CDR / framework region annotations.
features.evidence_summary: Apply Numbering Scheme and Immunum both assign antibody numbering (IMGT/Kabat/Chothia/AHo) and share the antibodyNumbering role; Manage Annotations exposes the resulting region annotations for review.

features.name: Liability Scanning
features.description: Scans macromolecule sequences for chemical liabilities such as deamidation and oxidation sites that affect biologic drug stability.
features.evidence_summary: Scan Liabilities is the sole explicit liability-scanning entry point, matching changelog entries about deamidation / oxidation detection and separator support.

features.name: VD Regions Viewer
features.description: Visualizes V-Domain antibody regions and CDR loops in a dedicated grid viewer, showing aligned region positions across a sequence library.
features.evidence_summary: VdRegions is the sole V-Domain regions viewer with role viewer,panel, described as 'V-Domain regions viewer' and referenced in the antibody support changelog entry.

features.name: Sequence Encoding for ML
features.description: Encodes macromolecule sequences as numeric feature vectors to feed into dimensionality reduction and other machine-learning pipelines.
features.evidence_summary: Encode Sequences carries the 'dim-red-preprocessing-function' role tag, explicitly marking it as the ML preprocessing step for sequence space and similar dim-red analyses.
